summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a combined zoning case hearing and regular meeting. The primary focus was zoning case ZC2023-007, a major modification to an existing Planned Unit Development (PUD) for a 225-home residential subdivision, which was approved with numerous conditions including traffic study, buffering, mounding, and emergency access requirements. The board also addressed financial matters including Ohio EMA reimbursement funds, approved flooring replacement for the administration building, authorized purchase of a police vehicle, administered the oath of office to Trustee Hausermann, and approved a collective bargaining agreement with the firefighters' union.
decisions: Approved major modification to PUD zoning case ZC2023-007 with conditions including final development plan requirements, tree survey, landscape plan, amenity details, building information, updated traffic impact study, buffer on emergency exit with swale and mound, double gates on emergency drive, mounding along M1/M2 property, and privacy fence in areas where mounding is insufficient
Approved increase in revenue of $482,950.52 from Ohio EMA for out-of-pocket expenses and mutual aid partners
Approved increase in appropriation of ARPA fund 2272-760-720-1229 in the amount of $13,905 for flooring removal and replacement
Approved Monster Coat to remove and replace flooring on lower level of administration building
Approved purchase of 2023 Ford Explorer Police Interceptor from Lebanon Ford for $41,680
Trustee Hausermann re-elected and administered Oath of Office
Approved entering executive session for employee discipline and bargaining session matters per ORC 121.22
Approved collective bargaining agreement between Goshen Township and Goshen Professional Firefighters Local 3932, effective January 1, 2024 through December 31, 2026
action_items: Developer (Robert Kerwin/Coach Development) to provide final development plan meeting Article 6 Section 602 requirements
Developer to work with certified urban landscape architect and/or foresters to prepare tree survey for the PUD site
Developer to provide landscape plan addressing screening between adjacent properties
Developer to update traffic impact study with ODOT (expected early February 2024)
Developer to implement mounding system along north property line (77 feet to 44 feet buffer areas) adjacent to M1/M2 properties
Developer to install double gates on emergency access drive
Developer to install privacy fence with pine trees where mounding is insufficient near M1/M2 property
Developer to address swale and buffer on south side (Pringle side) of emergency exit
Jen Farmer to continue pursuing additional Ohio EMA reimbursement checks through 2024
Fire department RFQ responses for Station 18 due December 19 from architects
Zoning staff to send updated notification letters to adjacent property owners for future meetings
key_amounts: $482,950.52 - Ohio EMA reimbursement check received for out-of-pocket expenses and mutual aid
$219,000 - Township portion of Ohio EMA reimbursement
$13,905 - ARPA appropriation for administration building flooring removal and replacement
$41,680 - 2023 Ford Explorer Police Interceptor from Lebanon Ford
1,750 square feet - area of flooring to be replaced in administration building lower level
225 homes - proposed residential development in PUD
Couple hundred thousand dollars - amount Carl Nause's family previously spent to widen and straighten Snyder Road
notable_topics: Zoning case ZC2023-007 - Major modification to Planned Unit Development for 225-home residential subdivision
Traffic safety concerns on State Route 48 and Short 48 from residents
Emergency access road design and proximity to adjacent property (Carl Pringle)
Buffer zones, mounding, and screening between PUD and adjacent M1/M2 industrial properties (Carl Nause)
Administration building lower level flooring replacement with polished concrete by Monster Coat
Trustee Hausermann oath of office re-election
Collective bargaining agreement with Goshen Professional Firefighters Local 3932 (2024-2026)
ODOT traffic impact study pending for subdivision entrance on Route 48
attendees: Carl Nause (Resident/Business Owner, 6468 Charles Snyder Road)
Jen Farmer (staff, Ohio EMA reimbursement work)
notes: The transcript has significant portions of garbled/corrupted text throughout (repeated 'Board of Trustees Meeting. Clermont County, Ohio 45122. Roads.' placeholder text), making some details difficult to capture. The PUD was originally approved in August 2018 with an extension granted in October 2019 but no development occurred. The developer referenced Ryan Holmes as the builder and mentioned a British golf course naming theme for streets. The executive session began and returned at 7:51 PM. The collective bargaining agreement was negotiated in-house without outside assistance, which the board noted positively. The meeting concluded with Christmas wishes.
title: BOT 12-12-23
